_meta.keyKnowledge: We describe a way to solve a problem we have seen before.

_source.w: We describe a way to solve a problem we have seen before.
y: Being able to explain how we solve problems shows we understand the process.
Describing strategies helps us remember and use them again.
Sharing problem-solving ideas helps others learn from us too.
eg: We explain to a friend how we solved a puzzle.
We tell our teacher the steps we used to fix something.
We describe what we tried first and what we tried next.
We use drawings or gestures to show our problem-solving approach.
We share our strategy with the class so others can try it.
We describe why we chose one approach over another.
